_README: Copy to models.json and edit. This file makes the system MODEL-AGNOSTIC: the workflows (MODES.md, multi-model/COUNCIL.md, estimate-cost.ts) refer to abstract ROLES, never to specific model names. You map each role to whatever model you actually have — any vendor (Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, xAI, a local model). Swap the whole roster by changing `active_roster`. JSON has no comments; every field is documented in MODELS.md.

_roles_doc.workhorse: Routine drafting and per-verse review. Mid capability, low cost. The tier you run most.
_roles_doc.deep: Hard verses (cruxes, poetry) + the referee/synthesis role + final review. High capability, higher cost.
_roles_doc.independent: A DIFFERENT VENDOR from your primary, used to mine cross-model disagreement (the old 'Gemini slot', generalized). Its value is that it fails in different places than your workhorse/deep — so where it disagrees is where a human should look. If your workhorse+deep are Anthropic, put a Google/OpenAI model here (and vice-versa).
_roles_doc.fast: OPTIONAL. Cheapest model, for mechanical sub-tasks only (never for generating the target language). Omit if you don't have one.
_roles_doc.local: OPTIONAL. An offline / zero-marginal-cost model (Ollama, llama.cpp). Omit if not used.

_entry_fields.provider: anthropic | google | openai | xai | local | other
_entry_fields.model: the model id your provider expects (these churn — set to what your account currently offers)
_entry_fields.capability: low | mid | high — used to keep deep > workhorse and to warn if you put a low model on a generation role
_entry_fields.cost_unit: relative per-agent output-price weight (drives estimate-cost.ts). Set Anthropic Sonnet=1 as the baseline and scale others to it.
_entry_fields.invoke: subagent (spawned inside a Claude Code session) | cli (you run a CLI and paste the result) | api (a script calls the API) | manual (you drive it in a chat window)
_entry_fields.cli: OPTIONAL command template for invoke=cli, e.g. 'gemini -p' — informational, the runner just needs to know how you'll call it

independent.notes: different vendor than workhorse/deep on purpose — the disagreement IS the finding

workhorse.notes: cheapest decent generator available to many teams
zero marginal cost; quality varies by language — verify hard with the free L0 tools

deep.notes: spend real inference only on the hard verses a local model can't carry

tiers._doc: Maps each inference tier (MODES.md) to a mix of roles. estimate-cost.ts reads this so the cost numbers, the docs, and the runner all share ONE source of truth. L0 is free (no agents) and is not listed. counts are agents-per-verse.
